topic.what: Evolution-aware cross-architecture vulnerability retrieval and patch-state profiling for stripped BusyBox-based IoT firmware
topic.how: Combines anonymous instruction/context features, graph-level statistics, per-binary geometric priors, and historical function prototypes to localize homologous vulnerable functions without symbols or source
topic.why_matters: Enables scalable IoT firmware vulnerability auditing when firmware images are stripped, vendor patches inconsistent, and same source compiled for heterogeneous architectures

characteristics.what_it_measures: Top-1 retrieval accuracy weighted across all versions
characteristics.evidence: Weighted Hit@1 across 57 versions and 1,020 directed architecture pairs
characteristics.context: Best on 56 of 57 versions, consistent on difficult architecture pairs

characteristics.what_it_measures: Top-10 retrieval accuracy weighted across all versions
characteristics.evidence: Weighted Hit@10 across 57 versions and 1,020 directed architecture pairs
characteristics.context: Maintains consistent advantages across architecture pairs

characteristics.evidence: 1 minus expected first-hit rank divided by candidate pool size
characteristics.context: Critical for large-scale firmware auditing workflows
Shows robust evolution-awareness across version changes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Vulnerable vs patched lineage classification accuracy
characteristics.context: Applied to AWK case study across architectures

characteristics.what_it_measures: Harmonic mean of precision and recall for patch state

novelty: evolution-aware binary retrieval framework for stripped IoT firmware
geometric shape priors combined with multi-view embeddings and historical prototypes
cross-version BusyBox benchmark with 57 versions and 155845 high-confidence stripped-to-unstripped matches
three-task formulation: retrieval, evolution-aware vulnerability search, patch-state profiling
open_problems: scalability to unpaired binaries without unstripped anchors
handling backported vendor patches that partially apply upstream fixes
dataset drift as BusyBox evolves over time
integration with broader firmware triage and recurring-vulnerability tracking workflows
